description:
Fix lifecycle hooks documentation to include the required `override` keyword on all code examples. Currently, all code examples in docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx omit `override` on lifecycle hook methods, but the scaffolded tsconfig.json has `noImplicitOverride: true` — so any developer who copies these examples verbatim will get TS4114 compiler errors. The resources-prompts docs already correctly document the `override` requirement for resources and prompts, but the lifecycle hooks page doesn't follow the same pattern.

Known LOW issues documented but not addressed by stories:
- `localPlugins.add` silently accepts nonexistent paths (only a dim warning)
- `npx create-opentabs-plugin` returns 404 (package is `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin` with restricted access)
- `npx opentabs-plugin build` outside a plugin dir gives 404 (binary name mismatch with npm package name)
- Doc TypeScript code examples in plugin-development.mdx use double quotes but scaffolded .prettierrc enforces singleQuote:true
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add override keyword to all lifecycle hook code examples
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the lifecycle hooks documentation code examples to include the `override` keyword so that I can copy them into my plugin without getting TS4114 compiler errors.
acceptanceCriteria: All lifecycle hook method implementations in code examples have the `override` keyword: `override onActivate()`, `override onDeactivate()`, `override onNavigate(url: string)`, `override onToolInvocationStart(toolName: string)`, `override onToolInvocationEnd(toolName: string, success: boolean, durationMs: number)`
The full example at the end of the page (AnalyticsPlugin class) has `override` on all 5 hook methods

A brief note is added near the top of the page (after the introductory paragraph or in the first code example section) explaining that `override` is required because lifecycle hooks are optional methods defined on the `OpenTabsPlugin` base class, and the recommended `tsconfig.json` enforces `noImplicitOverride: true`
The note follows the same style as the existing `override` documentation in docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx (lines 156-158 and 374-376)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx

Locations that need `override` added:
- Line 74: `onActivate()` → `override onActivate()`
- Line 85: `onDeactivate()` → `override onDeactivate()`
- Line 132: `onNavigate(url: string)` → `override onNavigate(url: string)`
- Line 169: `onToolInvocationStart(toolName: string)` → `override onToolInvocationStart(toolName: string)`
- Line 214: `onToolInvocationEnd(toolName: string, success: boolean, durationMs: number)` → `override onToolInvocationEnd(toolName: string, success: boolean, durationMs: number)`
- Lines 249-273 (full AnalyticsPlugin example): add `override` to all 5 hook methods (onActivate, onDeactivate, onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd)

For the explanatory note, follow the pattern in docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx lines 156-158:
"`override` is required because `resources` is an optional property with a default value on the base class — TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` rule enforces it. Omitting `override` causes a TS4114 type error at build time."

Adapt this for lifecycle hooks: explain that lifecycle hooks are optional methods with definitions on the base class. A good place for this note is right after the sentence on line 7 ("All hooks are optional methods on the `OpenTabsPlugin` base class — implement only the ones you need.") — append something like: "Because the scaffolded `tsconfig.json` enables `noImplicitOverride`, implementations must use the `override` keyword."
